qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: API clienti resource allows low-privilege arbitrary customer read and update
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding is a missing object-level authorization check in `repo/src/API/App/v1/Clienti.php`; the advisory is an authenticated SQL injection in Scadenzario bulk handling through `id_records[]` into `modules/scadenzario/bulk.php`, so the vulnerable code and root cause differ.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Global attachment IDOR in view.php exposes arbitrary uploaded files
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding is a missing authorization check in `repo/view.php` attachment retrieval by `file_id`; the advisory is an `id_records[]` SQL injection in Scadenzario bulk operations, so the endpoint, sink, and root cause are unrelated.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's authenticated Scadenzario bulk-operations SQL injection root cause. The session's qualifying findings are separate access-control issues in different files and endpoints.
evidence: The advisory describes a specific error-based SQL injection in `POST /actions.php?id_module=18` where user-controlled `id_records[]` values survive `array_clean()` and are concatenated into the `modules/scadenzario/bulk.php` `IN (...)` clause without integer validation.

`api_clienti_broken_object_authorization` instead reports broken object-level authorization in `repo/src/API/App/v1/Clienti.php` direct customer read/update handlers and does not involve Scadenzario, `id_records[]`, or SQL string concatenation.

`view_php_global_attachment_idor` instead reports missing authorization in `repo/view.php` attachment download/preview logic by `file_id` and does not reach the advisory's SQL sink or an equivalent vulnerable control flow.
